methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $14.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

data.narrative:
Skild AI is a Series C–stage AI & Software company founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, currently valued at $14.0B+ on $1.4B+ raised. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private peers spanning the sector, including Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C), Wayve ($8.6B, Series D), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B), and Nominal ($1.0B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's last post-money valuation of $14.0B and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to later-stage transitions, implying $23.8B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $7.1B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $39.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in capital intensity, technology maturity, and market opportunity within frontier AI.

These methods support a triangulated range of $6.0B (low) to $27.4B (high), with a median of $15.4B. The current $14.0B valuation sits near the analytical median, suggesting reasonable positioning within the derived range. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of stage-progression versus peer benchmarking given the limited and dispersed peer set, and whether frontier AI companies command sustained premiums relative to maturing software valuations.
